problem.title: Minimum Platforms Required
problem.description: Given arrival and departure times of all trains, find the minimum number of platforms required at a railway station so no train waits.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sorting and Two Pointers
solutions.description: Sort arrival and departure arrays independently, then use two pointers to simulate a timeline: if the next arrival comes before or at the next departure, a new platform is needed; otherwise a platform is freed. The maximum platform count seen during this simulation is the answer. The key insight is that sorting decouples the two events into two sorted streams that can be merged in O(n), similar to an interval scheduling problem.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def find_platform(arr: List[int], dep: List[int]) -> int:
    arr = sorted(arr)
    dep = sorted(dep)
    n = len(arr)
    platforms = result = 1
    i = 1
    j = 0
    while i < n and j < n:
        if arr[i] <= dep[j]:
            platforms += 1
            i += 1
        else:
            platforms -= 1
            j += 1
        result = max(result, platforms)
    return result
